description.content:
MarineTraffic Containers API is your way to access state-of-the-art real-time visibility for container logistics. Container Intelligence API
Insights on near real-time terminal congestion, container vessels scheduled and actual calls.
- Vessel schedules / actual calls: Waiting time, stay time, arrival and departure details. - Terminal Live Congestion: Vessels currently waiting, currently at berth and congestion index. - Terminal Performance Metrics: 7d average waiting and stay times, performance index. - Carrier / Services Information: List of carriers operating at the terminal and their services. Container Tracking API
Keep track of your shipments / containers from a bill of lading, booking number or container number.

For more info visit related documentation pages.

This solution allows you to use MTInbox api to :

Notes
  • Upload attachment
  • Get information of the current user
  • Compose and send email
  • Get accessible lines information
  • Get Address book contact details


For more info visit related documentation pages.

Get all historical positions of a single vessel for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Default resolution for returned positions is up to 2 minutes. Use "period" parameter to limit the frequency of positions
  • Weather data is returned once within each hour's results
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** speed over ground returned in (knots x10)
  • **TIMESTAMP** UTC second when the report was generated (0-59)
  • Historical positions are available since January 2015
  • **Hourly** and **daily** records are the first records received during the hour or the day respectively
  • When the **period** is **hourly**, the data field **HEADING** is not available for positions older than 2017-11-17
  • **Hourly** records are available for dates after 2014-08-28
  • Weather data for **winds** are available for dates after 2015-05-27
  • Weather data for **waves**, **swells** and **currents** are available for dates after 2021-01-21
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

(Required) A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track
(Required) The number of days, starting from the time of request and going backwards, for which the response should look for position data </br></br> Maximum value is 190 (days)
Use with todate instead of days to get data for a date period
Use with fromdate instead of days to get data for a date period

Limit position per vessel. Omit to get all the available positions

Values are:
  • hourly: get one position per hour
  • daily: get one position per day

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also weather data

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

item.name: Vessel Historical Positions in an Area

request.name: Vessel Historical Positions in an Area

description.content:
Get vessel historical positions for a single or for all vessels inside an area of your preference for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Default resolution for returned positions is up to 2 minutes. Use "period" parameter to limit the frequency of positions
  • Weather data is returned once within each hour's results
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** speed over ground returned in (knots x10)
  • **TIMESTAMP** UTC second when the report was generated (0-59)
  • Historical positions are available since January 2015
  • **Hourly** and **daily** records are the first records received during the hour or the day respectively
  • When the **period** is **hourly**, the data field **HEADING** is not available for positions older than 2017-11-17
  • **Hourly** records are available for dates after 2014-08-28
  • Weather data for **winds** are available for dates after 2015-05-27
  • Weather data for **waves**, **swells** and **currents** are available for dates after 2021-01-21
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest
(Required) The number of days, starting from the time of request and going backwards, for which the response should look for position data </br></br> Maximum value is 190 (days)
Use with todate instead of days to get data for a date period
Use with fromdate instead of days to get data for a date period

(Required) Use with MAXLAT, MINLON, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

(Required) Use with MINLAT, MINLON, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

(Required) Use with MINLAT, MAXLAT, MAXLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

(Required) Use with MINLAT, MAXLAT, MINLON to define an area for which you wish to either get all the historical vessels' positions or historical positions for a specific vessel

The absolute value of MAXLAT - MINLAT + MAXLON - MINLON has to be equal to or less than 2

If you do not define a vessel, the maximum period for which you can look back has a maximum period of one day

A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

Limit position per vessel. Omit to get all the available positions

Values are:
  • hourly: get one position per hour
  • daily: get one position per day

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also weather data

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

item.name: Vessel Positions of a Static Fleet

request.name: Vessel Positions of a Static Fleet

description.content:
Keep a close eye on the vessel or group of vessels of your interest.

Notes
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** returned in (knots x10) and **TIMESTAMP** in UTC
  • **HEADING** for SAR Aircrafts contains the Altitude of the aircraft (“STATUS” = 97)
  • **HEADING** values -1 or 511 indicate lack of data
  • **SPEED** and **COURSE** values for base stations are represented by zero (0)
  • More information about response parameters: STATUS, SHIPTYPE
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 8 to get the latest
The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned positions.  </br></br> Maximum value for terrestrial coverage is 60. Maximum value for satellite coverage is 180

Filter data by vessel type:
  • 2: Fishing
  • 4: High Speed Craf
  • 6: Passenger
  • 7: Cargo
  • 8: Tanker

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
  • full
If used with the value extended or full, the response includes scheduled static and voyage related vessel data report (AIS Message 5). In this case your request frequency might be limited (depending on your service terms)

If omitted, the returned records include only position reports (AIS Messages 1, 2, 3/ 18, 19)

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

description.content:
The latest version of this API endpoint, including significant improvements, is available here.

Please contact your Customer Success Manager to request access to the new endpoint.



Track vessels of interest anywhere in the world.

Notes
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **SPEED** speed over ground returned in (knots x10)
  • **SPEED** and **COURSE** values for base stations are represented by zero (0)
  • **TIMESTAMP** UTC second when the report was generated (0-59)
  • **HEADING** true heading in degrees (0-359) (values -1 or 511 indicate lack of data)
  • **HEADING** for SAR Aircrafts contains the Altitude of the aircraft (“STATUS” = 97)
  • **DSRC** describes whether the transmitted AIS message was received by a terrestrial (TER), satellite (SAT) or roaming (ROAM) AIS antenna.
  • The timespan parameter lets you specify how far back in time the API should look for the latest available vessel positions. Default value is set to 5 minutes and can be adjusted to a maximum of 1,440 minutes (24 hours).
  • More information about response parameters: STATUS, SHIPTYPE
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Use latest version **9**
Overrides the default timespan

Filter vessels based on vessel types, comma separated ids supported

more
The pagination cursor provided in the metadata section of the previous response
The limit of vessels per page (min=1000, max=5000)

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • jsono
  • csv

Notes

response.name: Bad Request

description.content:
⚠️This is a legacy endpoint.

A new and improved version is now available, offering enhanced data quality, latest features and better performance. We strongly recommend switching to the latest version, which you can explore here: Vessel Particulars & Ownership.

If you would like to see the new endpoint in action, feel free to request a demo - our team would be happy to walk you through it.

Retrieve particulars for one or more vessels.

Notes
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • If page parameter is missing, then default page:1 will be used instead
  • The results returned from the API call are split into pages of 100 rows
  • In order to get each page generated by the call, add the /page:#value/ parameter to the URL. The default value is /page:1/
  • The API does not return results for vessels that are not IMO-registered
  • The API does not return results for dead vessels
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 5 to get the latest

(Required) The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track (single or comma separated values)

You can instead use imo or shipid
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track (single or comma separated values)
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel (single or comma separated values)

The timespan parameter defines the period of time, during which we want to receive the change in a vessel's details </br></br> The maximum value can be either 5 when the interval parameter is set to days or 2880 when the interval is set to minutes
The interval parameter can be either minutes or days

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono
Page number of the results to be returned

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended

description.content:
Get ETA and voyage information for a vessel of your choice, to any port you define.

Notes
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 2 to get the latest

(Required) The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to receive ETA info

You can instead use unlocode
The UN LOCODE (without gaps or spaces) of the port you wish to receive ETA info

(Required) The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

You can instead use imo or shipid
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

The Speed of the Vessel that MarineTraffic will use to produce the ETA value
  • if not defined the last average speed of the Vessel will be used
  • if no average speed exists then a default value of 12(kn) will be used

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

description.content:
Get expected arrivals to a specific port.

Notes
  • **SPEED** returned in (knots x10) and **TIMESTAMP** in UTC
  • Use either **TIMESPAN** or **FROMDATE/TODATE** in order to look for expected arrivals in a specific date range
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 4 to get the latest
(Required) The MarineTraffic ID of the port (found on the URL of the respective Port page)

Number of days in the future to look for expected arrivals, starting from the time the API call is requested </br></br> Maximum value is 40 days </br></br> If neither this nor the fromdate/todate parameters are used, the response only includes vessels currently in port (or the country’s ports) and the last vessel's signal is received during the last 2 days

Include vessels currently in port that have transmitted a position within the last x days (based on their last signal timestamp). </br></br> This parameter only works when no other timeframe is defined.
Timestamp in the future to start looking for expected arrivals. If used, then you should also define the todate parameter </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
Timestamp in the future to start looking for expected arrivals. If used, then you should also define the fromdate parameter </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
The MarineTraffic ID of the previous port (found on the URL of the respective Port page) or previous port UNLOCODE

Filter data by vessel type:
  • 2: Fishing
  • 4: High Speed Craf
  • 6: Passenger
  • 7: Cargo
  • 8: Tanker
Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8). If used, then you should also define the dwt_max parameter
Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8). If used, then you should also define the dwt_min parameter
Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels. If used, then you should also define the gt_max parameter
Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels. If used, then you should also define the gt_min parameter

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, apart from the ETA information, the response includes main vessel particulars. If omitted, the returned records include only position and ETA reports

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

Include vessels currently in port that have transmitted a position within the last x days (based on their last signal timestamp). </br></br> This parameter only works when no other timeframe is defined.

query.description: (Required) The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to receive probable visits (found on the URL of the respective Port page)

Define market for which you would like to receive probable visits - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • If defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market
Define ship class of interest - more
Define probability over which you would like to receive probable visits (accepted > 0.3)

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

description.content:
Receive the Port Congestion for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • More information about input parameters: MARKET, SHIPCLASS
  • **NOT ALLOWED** combinations in request
    • agg_market:1 **and** agg_shipclass:0
    • agg_market:1 **and** agg_port:1
    • agg_shipclass:1 **and** agg_port:1
    • agg_market:1 **and** shipclass (specific value in shipclass e.g. POST PANAMAX)
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description:
(Required) The MarineTraffic ID of the port for which you wish to retrieve congestion metrics (found on the URL of the respective Port page).

If you wish to retrieve congestion metrics for all ports, instead of portid use agg_port=1
(Required) Year of interest (data available back to 1 year). Information further back can be requested ad-hoc
(Required) Week of Year that is of interest (e.g. for week 18/2018 input 18). For range of weeks use comma e.g. for weeks 30 to 35 (30,35)

(Required) Define market for which you would like to receive port congestion - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then figures for ALL markets/ship-class
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, returns based on ship-class for ALL applicable to size class markets
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, returns figures for the specific market (no size class breakdown)


If you wish to retrieve congestion metrics for all markets, instead of market use agg_market query parameter (=0 or =1)

(Required) Define ship class of interest - more

If you wish to retrieve congestion metrics for all ship classes, instead of shipclass use agg_shipclass query parameter (=0 or =1)

**If used, portid should not be in request**
  • when agg_port = 1 is used, aggregation result of all ports are returned
  • when agg_port = 1 is used, agg_market cannot be 1

**If used, market should not be in request**
  • if agg_market = 1, aggregation result of all markets will be returned
  • if 0, a breakdown of markets will be returned

**If used, shipclass should not be in request**
  • if agg_shipclass = 1, aggregation result of all classes will be returned
  • if 0, a breakdown of classes will be returned

The median number of days spent at anchorage by the selected market/shipclass. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Comma separated float numbers (one decimal)

Available only if extended version of the API is used

The median number of days spent at port by the selected market/shipclass. Values indicate in between set to be returned

Number of individual calls that were used in the median calculations (e.g. if the same vessel has called twice within the same week, it will count twice in calls and once in vessels. Value of parameter indicates greater than relation

description.content:
A newer version of this endpoint is available here: https://developers.kpler.com/spec/c78f79cf-4169-4a59-987d-e5970ea170f9

Receive a list of available routes and distances from a vessel to a port.

Notes
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description:
(Required) The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

You can instead use imo or shipid
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel
(Required) The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to put as target port (found on the URL of the respective Port page) or port UN/LOCODE
Use 1 to search for all available routes regarding the specific journey or 0 to not include alternative routes
Use 1 in order to receive routes which include inland waterways or 0 to not include alternative routes

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes also route/waypoints as Linestring Geometry in WKT - Well-Known Text

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • json
  • jsono

description.content:
Manage your MarineTraffic fleet.

To **create a new item** in a fleet, use the active parameter. If the item already exists, it will **update** item's status.

The active parameter can have values of 0, 1, 2 which corresponds to the three states of the fleet items (inactive, active, sat enabled)

The **delete** parameter must have a value of 1 and it **removes** the entry from the fleet.

If MMSI, IMO and SHIP ID are passed, the MMSI takes precedence and the IMO and SHIP ID will be ignored.

When you add vessels to a Fleet of Interest using the setfleet API endpoint:
  • If you provide an IMO or MMSI that matches multiple vessels in the MarineTraffic database, the system will automatically select the vessel with the most recent AIS timestamp and add it to your fleet.
  • If you provide a shipid, the system will add that exact vessel — no duplicate resolution is performed.
  • To avoid any ambiguity, we recommend using shipid when you want precise control over which vessel is added.
Response is always an XML structure indicating success or failure with failure description message.

Notes
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description:
(Required) The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to handle, comma separated values supported

You can instead use imo or shipid
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to handle, comma separated values supported
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel, comma separated values supported
If used, vessel will be added to the specific fleet, otherwise default fleet is used

Possible values are:
  • 0 to set to inactive
  • 1 to activate
  • 2 to enable satellite coverage
Set this parameter to 1 to delete a vessel from your fleet

description.content:

If used, will return specific fleet otherwise default fleet is used
Specifies the number of the results page to return. Use this parameter to navigate through paginated results (with version 2) when the fleet contains more than 100 vessels.

Import a passage plan for one of your managed vessels.

Notes
  • Passage plans can only be imported for vessels that belong to user's managed vessels (see Fleet Operations solution for more info)
  • Accepted file formats are CSV, RTZ or RT3
  • Maximum file size supported is 20MB
  • File should contain at least 2 waypoints
  • Imported passage plans overwrite previously imported ones for the provided api_key and IMO combination
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.
(Required) API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

Complete MarineTraffic AIS Data API covering vessel positions, events, vessels data, voyage information, ports information, routing, reverse geocoding, and power-user fleet/balance services. Backed by 13,000+ AIS receivers operated by Kpler / MarineTraffic.
